steps.title: Count the number of doubling periods
steps.content:
From 1659 to 1959 is 1959 − 1659 = 300 years.
Since the population doubles every 75 years, the number of doubling periods is $$\frac{300}{75} = 4$$

steps.title: Set up the exponential growth equation
steps.content: When a population doubles every time period, after $n$ periods the population is: $$P = P_0 \cdot 2^n$$ where $P_0$ is the initial population. With 4 doublings: $$240,000 = P_0 \cdot 2^4$$

steps.title: Solve for the 1659 population
steps.content: Calculate $2^4 = 16$, then divide both sides by 16: $$P_0 = \frac{240,000}{16} = 15,000$$ The population in 1659 was 15,000.
